records.ety: [L. idyllium, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ form; literally, a little form of image: cf. F. idylle. See Idol.]

defs: A short poem; properly, a short pastoral poem; as, the idyls of Theocritus; also, any poem, especially a narrative or descriptive poem, written in an elevated and highly finished style; also, by extension, any artless and easily flowing description, either in poetry or prose, of simple, rustic life, of pastoral scenes, and the like.
quotes: His [Goldsmith's] lovely idyl of the Vicar's home.
